title: Constellations in the Sky
subtitle: Location-aware sky map from real star positions
viewNote: Stars, constellation lines, and optional boundaries rendered locally with VirtualSky.

zodiacNotes.Ari: Aries is a small northern zodiac constellation. Its modest pattern sits between Pisces and Taurus and marks the Ram in the traditional zodiac.
zodiacNotes.Tau: Taurus is one of the richest zodiac constellations, with Aldebaran, the Hyades, and the Pleiades all nearby.
zodiacNotes.Gem: Gemini is anchored by Castor and Pollux, the twin stars that make this winter constellation easy to recognize.
zodiacNotes.Cnc: Cancer is faint, but it contains the Beehive Cluster, a bright open cluster visible under dark skies.
zodiacNotes.Leo: Leo is marked by Regulus and a sickle-shaped head. It is one of spring's most recognizable zodiac constellations.
zodiacNotes.Vir: Virgo is the largest zodiac constellation. Its bright star Spica sits near the ecliptic path of the Moon and planets.
zodiacNotes.Lib: Libra represents the scales. Its main stars form a compact shape between Virgo and Scorpius.
zodiacNotes.Sco: Scorpius is a dramatic zodiac constellation with the red star Antares and a long curved tail.
zodiacNotes.Sgr: Sagittarius lies toward the center of the Milky Way. Its teapot-shaped star pattern is a useful summer guide.
zodiacNotes.Cap: Capricornus is a faint but ancient zodiac constellation, often drawn as a sea-goat in classical sky lore.
zodiacNotes.Aqr: Aquarius covers a broad, mostly faint region of the zodiac and is associated with the Water Bearer.
zodiacNotes.Psc: Pisces is a large, faint constellation of two fish joined by a cord, stretching across the autumn sky.
